title: Business and financial project management professionals
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate marketing policies and programs, such as determining the demand for products and services offered by a firm and its competitors, and identify potential customers. Develop pricing strategies with the goal of maximizing the firm's profits or share of the market while ensuring the firm's customers are satisfied. Oversee product development or monitor trends that indicate the need for new products and services. Plan, direct, or coordinate the actual distribution or movement of a product or service to the customer. Coordinate sales distribution by establishing sales territories, quotas, and goals and establish training programs for sales representatives. Analyze sales statistics gathered by staff to determine sales potential and inventory requirements and monitor the preferences of customers. Plan, direct, or coordinate activities designed to create or maintain a favorable public image or raise issue awareness for their organization or client. (Based on 41 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Analyze financial data, including credit data and financial statements, to determine risk involved in extending credit or lending money and generate financial ratios to evaluate customers' financial status. Prepare reports regarding market conditions, merchandise costs, and the expected profitability of loans. Review financial statements and sales reports to identify opportunities for cost reductions or service improvements. Set marketing, sales, or other financial goals and monitor progress toward these goals. Conduct market research and analyze industry trends. Plan and implement changes to service offerings to meet community needs or increase revenues.

Consult with customers to resolve complaints and verify financial and credit transactions. Evaluate customer records to recommend payment plans based on earnings, savings data, payment history, and purchase activity. Review individual or commercial customer files to identify and select delinquent accounts for collection. Respond to customer complaints and negotiate payment negotiations or other post-service matters.

Conduct classes to teach procedures to staff and oversee employee training and performance evaluations. Interview and hire staff, and schedule work hours for employees. Identify skill development needs for staff and prepare employee work schedules. Communicate goals, policies, or procedures to staff members.

Develop and implement purchasing and contract management instructions, policies, and procedures. Review purchase order claims and contracts for conformance to company policy. Negotiate contracts for services and represent companies in negotiating policies with suppliers. Locate vendors of materials or supplies, and prepare and process requisitions and purchase orders for contracts, equipment, and supplies. Evaluate the performance of vendors and direct purchasing department budgets.

Oversee the maintenance and repair operations or administrative services for funeral homes to ensure compliance with regulations. Monitor operations to ensure quality and efficiency and maintain records of goods ordered and received. Review financial reports to ensure efficiency of operations and prepare operational reports and schedules.

Participate in the development and implementation of marketing strategies to create or maintain a favorable public image or raise awareness for the organization. Attend or make presentations at community events to promote services and build community relationships. Communicate security status, updates and potential problems using established protocols.

Conduct investigations to ensure legality of transactions and operations or financial solvency. Analyze market and delivery systems to assess current and future material availability. Investigate activities of institutions to enforce laws and regulations and ensure compliance with policies. Review applications for mergers or acquisitions and prepare reports, including recommendations for actions to ensure compliance with laws and regulations.

Remove irrelevant core tasks related to delivering death certificates, preparing loan applications, and overseeing administrative or logistical functions unrelated to business or financial project management. Tasks #0, #1, #10, #4, #56, #134, #58, #49, #49, #59, #144, #145, #146, #147, #149.

Confer with clients to develop plans for posttreatment activities, and educate clients or community members about mental or physical illness, abuse, medication, or available community resources. Counsel clients on various concerns, including relationships, divorce, child rearing, home management, and financial difficulties. Assist clients in adhering to treatment plans and encourage them to develop and use skills for confronting their problems. Analyze information from interviews, educational and medical records, and diagnostic evaluations to assess clients' needs and develop individualized treatment plans.

Monitor customer preferences to determine the focus of sales efforts and resolve customer complaints regarding sales and service. Develop pricing strategies that balance firm objectives with customer satisfaction, and evaluate the financial aspects of product development, including budgets and return-on-investment projections. Formulate and coordinate marketing activities or policies to promote products or services, and use sales forecasting or strategic planning to ensure the sale and profitability of products or services.

Collaborate with other professionals, such as physicians and mental health professionals, to evaluate patients' medical or physical conditions and coordinate treatment efforts. Refer clients to community resources for services and advocate for them to resolve crises. Develop and implement treatment plans and rehabilitation programs based on research and clinical experience.

Supervise or direct other workers providing services to clients or patients, and coordinate or participate in training programs for individuals or group sessions. Develop and implement follow-up or aftercare programs for clients discharged from treatment programs, while maintaining confidentiality of records and preparing necessary reports.

Collect information about clients through various methods, including testing and observation, to create comprehensive case files and records. Conduct evaluations to ensure that treatment plans appropriately address changes in client status and needs. Plan and conduct structured counseling programs and group sessions that target social issues and promote health or therapy.

Develop relationships with community referral sources and collaborate with community agencies to establish programs for individuals facing challenges. Advocate for clients to help them overcome barriers to employment and improve their social conditions through community resources. Participate in case conferences and team meetings to ensure comprehensive care coordination.

Follow up on the effectiveness of counseling programs and client adjustments, including monitoring clients' use of medications and supporting them in addressing their substance abuse or addictive behaviors. Conduct crisis interventions and implement strategies to resolve problems that arise during treatment. Adapt treatment activities based on clients’ evolving needs.

Conduct market research studies or analyze findings to identify potential markets and recommend environmental marketing strategies. Oversee promotional activities and manage logistics for product distribution to maximize market reach while ensuring environmental sustainability.

Plan or conduct business-related programs, including community health or substance abuse prevention initiatives. Engage in social research to enhance knowledge and inform community development policies, working alongside other social service agencies.

Complete and maintain comprehensive documentation and records regarding clients' histories and progress, ensuring compliance with mandated reports and confidentiality requirements. Develop community outreach materials to facilitate access to social service agencies and resources.

Provide necessary evaluations and assessments to determine the appropriateness of referrals for clients, including academic, vocational, and rehabilitation needs. Collaborate closely with clients and their families to address personal and social issues based on the assessment results.

Review operational records and reports to project sales and determine profitability. Direct, coordinate, and review sales and service accounting and record-keeping, as well as receiving and shipping. Prepare budgets and approve budget expenditures. Oversee regional and local sales managers and their staffs. Analyze data gathered and develop solutions or alternative methods of proceeding. Develop cost estimates, forecasts, or cost models.

Confer or consult with department heads to plan advertising services and to secure information on equipment and customer specifications. Confer with personnel concerned to ensure successful functioning of newly implemented systems or procedures. Communicate with or monitor service providers, such as ocean carriers, air freight forwarders, global consolidators, customs brokers, or trucking companies. Maintain and develop positive business relationships with a customer's key personnel involved in logistics activities. Develop an understanding of customers' needs and take actions to ensure those needs are met. Direct availability and allocation of materials, supplies, and finished products. Collaborate with other departments as necessary to meet customer requirements or capitalize on sales opportunities.

Plan, organize, and execute logistics support activities, such as maintenance planning, repair analysis, and test equipment recommendations. Direct team activities, establishing task priorities, scheduling and tracking work assignments, providing guidance, and ensuring the availability of resources. Report project plans, progress, and results. Develop proposals that include documentation for estimates, and prepare manuals and train workers in the use of new forms, reports, procedures, or equipment.

Conduct logistics studies or analyses to identify developments in logistics planning or execution. Analyze logistics data, using methods such as data mining or cost-benefit analysis. Develop or document procedures to minimize or mitigate carbon output resulting from the movement of materials or products. Propose logistics solutions for customers and recommend improvements to existing or planned logistics processes.

Prepare reports on logistics performance measures and review logistics performance with customers against targets. Monitor industry standards, trends, or practices to identify developments in logistics planning or execution. Maintain logistics records in accordance with corporate policies. Maintain databases of logistics information. Develop logistic metrics, internal analysis tools, or key performance indicators for business units.

Develop or maintain models for logistics uses, such as cost estimating or demand forecasting. Plan study of work problems and procedures, such as organizational change or inventory control. Determine requirements for compliance with environmental certification standards and review global, national, or regional transportation reports for ways to improve efficiency or minimize the environmental impact of logistics activities. Identify opportunities for inventory reductions.

Conduct prehearing and presentencing investigations, and testify in court regarding offenders' backgrounds and recommended sentences. Counsel clients or patients, individually or in groups, to assist in overcoming dependencies or adjusting to life. Provide clients or family members with information about addiction issues and about available services. Interview clients individually, in families, or in groups to assess their situations and determine what services are required.

[REDACTED] Coordinate activities with courts, probation officers, community services, or other post-treatment agencies. [REDACTED] Identify and approve work placements for offenders with community service sentences. [REDACTED] Develop liaisons and networks with other parole officers and community agencies to plan for helping offenders with life adjustments. [REDACTED] Provide offenders or inmates with assistance in matters concerning detainers, sentences in other jurisdictions, writs, and applications for social assistance. [REDACTED] Recommend appropriate penitentiary for initial placement of an offender. [REDACTED] Recommend remedial action or initiate court action in response to noncompliance with terms of probation or parole. [REDACTED] Assess the suitability of penitentiary inmates for release under parole and statutory release programs. [REDACTED] Remotely monitor the flow of vehicles or inventory, using Web-based logistics information systems. [REDACTED] Analyze financial or operational performance of companies facing financial difficulties to identify or recommend remedies.

[REMOVED] Collaborate with investment bankers to attract new corporate clients. [REMOVED] Evaluate and compare the relative quality of various securities in a given industry. [REMOVED] Purchase investments for companies in accordance with company policy. [REMOVED] Monitor developments in the fields of industrial technology and economic theory. [REMOVED] Prepare all materials for transactions or execution of deals. [REMOVED] Prepare plans of action for investment. [REMOVED] Present oral or written reports on general economic trends. [REMOVED] Assess companies as investments for clients by examining company facilities.

Manage special events, such as sponsorship of races or parties introducing new products, to gain public attention, and develop and implement communication plans tailored to key client groups and audiences.
Formulate policies and procedures related to public information and fundraising programs, including evaluating compatibility with advertising and promotion efforts.
Establish and maintain effective working relationships with clients, government officials, and media representatives to develop new business and fundraising opportunities.
Direct activities of external agencies that implement communication and fundraising strategies, and manage communications budgets.
Confer with labor relations managers to develop internal communications that keep employees informed, and facilitate consumer relations within the company.
Draft speeches for executives and prepare media kits, including writing effective press releases and developing internet or intranet web pages.
Identify potential clients and develop strategies to increase contributions, while planning fundraising activities that maximize participation and minimize costs.
Evaluate fundraising advertising programs for compatibility with public relations efforts and maintain documentation of compliance activities.
Conduct research to identify funding organization goals and donor histories, and compile materials for submission to granting organizations.
Analyze financial information to inform decisions and provide statistical modeling advice related to fundraising and risk management.
Prepare plans of action for projects and manage or oversee operational activities, ensuring compliance with applicable health, safety, or hygiene standards.
Develop strategies and marketing campaigns that promote the organization and its fundraising initiatives, while assessing program effectiveness.
Gather and analyze risk-related data, evaluate relevant investments, and document key risks associated with potential fundraising activities.
Oversee the development of promotional materials, ensuring design and messaging align with organizational goals and enhance public image.

Advise internal management or business partners on the implementation or operation of compliance programs, and ensure that management and employees are informed about compliance reporting systems, policies, and practices.
Design or implement improvements in communication, monitoring, or enforcement of compliance standards, as well as plans for environmental compliance or reporting tools.

Manage warranty repair or replacement services, track cost-containment strategies and programs to evaluate effectiveness, and maintain records of decisions, actions, and progress related to environmental redevelopment projects.
Provide technical support to customers, employees, or subcontractors, and train or coordinate the training of employees in operations, safety, environmental issues, or technical issues.
Monitor compliance to operational, safety, or inventory control procedures, including physical security standards, and conduct or direct internal investigations of compliance issues.
Advise retail managers on compliance with applicable codes, laws, regulations, or standards, and represent organizations before regulatory agencies on product-related matters.
Analyze retail data to identify current or emerging trends in theft or fraud and investigate or interview individuals suspected of shoplifting or internal theft.
Coordinate internal investigations of loss prevention issues and develop or maintain partnerships with law enforcement to address theft or fraud cases.
Oversee the maintenance of operations records, such as work orders, site inspection forms, or compliance documentation, and ensure adherence to environmental regulations where applicable.
Develop risk management strategies based on assessments of product, compliance, or operational risks and monitor emerging trends regarding industry regulations to determine potential impacts.
Prepare detailed documentation for regulatory compliance, including permit applications and proposals for environmental remediation projects.
Supervise subcontractors or consultants to ensure quality and conformance to specifications and budgets.
Create project plans, including scope, goals, tasks, resources, schedules, costs, and contingency planning for various operational initiatives.
Estimate costs for operations and prepare wind energy operational budgets. Monitor costs incurred by project staff to identify budget issues.
Coordinate activities designed to create or maintain a favorable public image or raise issue awareness for their organization or client through reporting and communication efforts.
Develop procedures or systems for compliance submission in hardcopy or electronic formats.
Review or evaluate environmental remediation project proposals and designs for contaminant treatment or disposal facilities.
Identify potential sources of loss and develop strategies to eliminate it while overseeing loss prevention audit programs.
